title: pilosella bauhini subsp. arvorum
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. arvorum, also known as Devil's-bit scabious, is a perennial plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. arvorum include its potential to support respiratory health, reduce symptoms of allergies and hay fever, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
